meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/entra) — drafted from docs; org-specific values (admin auth strength, sign-in frequency) reviewed and confirmed 2026-06-27
generalNotes: All policies are created at Entra ID > Protection > Conditional Access > Policies > New policy.
Build each policy in Report-only first. Review Sign-in logs / policy impact, then switch Enable policy from Report-only to On.
Always exclude break-glass / emergency-access accounts (and any directory-sync or service accounts) under Users > Exclude so a misconfiguration can't lock everyone out.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Exclude
requiredSettings.value: Break-glass / emergency-access accounts; directory-sync service accounts

requiredSettings.label: Grant
requiredSettings.value: Grant access → Require authentication strength: Multifactor authentication (or the Inforcer MFA authentication strength)

steps: New policy → give it a clear name (e.g. 'CA01 - Require MFA - Internal Users').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Resources → Include = All resources (formerly 'All cloud apps').
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → select Require authentication strength = Multifactor authentication; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. After reviewing impact, switch to On.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Include
requiredSettings.value: Directory roles → the privileged admin roles (Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator, Security Administrator, Exchange/SharePoint Administrator, Conditional Access Administrator, etc.)

requiredSettings.label: Grant
requiredSettings.value: Grant access → Require authentication strength → select the Inforcer Authentication Strength (custom admin strength)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA02 - Require strong MFA - Admins').
Assignments → Users → Include = Directory roles; select the privileged admin roles; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → Require authentication strength → select the Inforcer Authentication Strength policy; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. Confirm admins can still sign in, then switch to On.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Include
requiredSettings.value: Guest or external users → Local guest users, Service provider users, and Other external users (do NOT tick B2B collaboration guest users — that is handled by the separate B2B policy)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA03 - Require MFA - Guests (non-B2B)').
Assignments → Users → Include → Select 'Guest or external users', then tick Local guest, Service provider, and Other external users; leave B2B collaboration guest users unticked.
Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → Require multifactor authentication; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create; switch to On after validation.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Include
requiredSettings.value: Guest or external users → B2B collaboration guest users (and B2B collaboration member users if used)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA04 - Require MFA - B2B Guests').
Assignments → Users → Include → 'Guest or external users' → tick B2B collaboration guest users.
Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → Require multifactor authentication; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create; switch to On after validation.
Note: home-tenant MFA satisfies this when cross-tenant access settings are configured to trust the guest's MFA.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Include
requiredSettings.value: All users (or the privileged admin roles)

requiredSettings.label: Grant
requiredSettings.value: Grant access → Require multifactor authentication (or Require authentication strength: Multifactor authentication)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA05 - Require MFA - Admin Portals').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include → Select resources → choose 'Microsoft Admin Portals'.
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → Require multifactor authentication; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create; switch to On after validation.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Exclude
requiredSettings.value: Break-glass accounts; any account that must keep legacy auth (audit regularly)

requiredSettings.label: Conditions — Client apps
requiredSettings.value: Configure = Yes; tick ONLY 'Exchange ActiveSync clients' and 'Other clients'

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA06 - Block - Legacy authentication').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts (plus any account that genuinely still needs legacy auth).
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Conditions → Client apps → Configure = Yes → check only 'Exchange ActiveSync clients' and 'Other clients'; Done.
Access controls → Grant → Block access; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. Use the Sign-ins-using-legacy-authentication workbook to confirm impact, then switch to On.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Exclude
requiredSettings.value: Break-glass accounts; documented device-code exceptions (e.g. Teams Room resource accounts)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA07 - Block - Device code flow').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts and any approved device-code exception group.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Conditions → Authentication Flows → Configure = Yes → select 'Device code flow'; Done.
Access controls → Grant → Block access; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. If using Teams devices/Azure CLI, scope exceptions and exclude the Device Registration Service resource, then switch to On.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Include
requiredSettings.value: All users (or the groups you want to block from transferring auth PC→mobile)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA08 - Block - Authentication transfer').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Conditions → Authentication Flows → Configure = Yes → select 'Authentication transfer'; Done.
Access controls → Grant → Block access; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create; switch to On after validation.

requiredSettings.label: Prerequisites
requiredSettings.value: Entra ID P1; Entra joined / hybrid joined / Entra registered Windows 10+ devices. Confirm app compatibility and that no VDI is in scope before enforcing.

requiredSettings.label: Target resources
requiredSettings.value: Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams (the resources Token Protection supports)

requiredSettings.label: Conditions — Client apps
requiredSettings.value: Configure = Yes; tick ONLY 'Mobile apps and desktop clients' (leave Browser unchecked — browser apps aren't supported)

requiredSettings.label: Session
requiredSettings.value: Require token protection for sign-in sessions

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA09 - Require token protection - Windows').
Assignments → Users → Include = pilot group / All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = select Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, and Microsoft Teams.
Conditions → Device platforms → Configure = Yes → Include = Windows.
Conditions → Client apps → Configure = Yes → under Modern authentication clients tick only 'Mobile apps and desktop clients'; Done.
Access controls → Session → tick 'Require token protection for sign-in sessions'; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. Use Sign-in logs (Token Protection - Sign-In Session = Bound) to confirm, then switch to On.
Pair with the 'Block unsupported platforms' policy so the control can't be bypassed from a spoofed platform.

requiredSettings.label: Users — Include
requiredSettings.value: All users (the population you enroll into Intune)

requiredSettings.label: Session — Sign-in frequency
requiredSettings.value: Periodic reauthentication, value per the Inforcer baseline (commonly a short window so enrollment uses a fresh, MFA-backed token)

requiredSettings.label: Grant
requiredSettings.value: Grant access → Require multifactor authentication (so reauth is MFA-backed)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA10 - Sign-in frequency - Intune Enrollment').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include → Select resources → 'Microsoft Intune Enrollment'.
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → Require multifactor authentication; Select.
Access controls → Session → tick 'Sign-in frequency' → Periodic reauthentication → set the value to the Inforcer baseline; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create; switch to On after validation.

requiredSettings.label: Prerequisites
requiredSettings.value: Matching Intune App Protection Policy (APP/MAM) targeting the same users; devices registered in Entra ID (Authenticator on iOS, Company Portal on Android act as broker).

requiredSettings.label: Conditions — Device platforms
requiredSettings.value: Configure = Yes; Include = Android and iOS

requiredSettings.label: Grant
requiredSettings.value: Grant access → Require app protection policy (and Require approved client app); 'Require one of the selected controls'

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA11 - Require app protection policy - Mobile').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Conditions → Device platforms → Configure = Yes → Include → select Android and iOS; Done.
Access controls → Grant → Grant access → tick 'Require app protection policy' and 'Require approved client app' → 'Require one of the selected controls'; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. Confirm a matching Intune App Protection Policy is assigned, then switch to On.

requiredSettings.label: Target resources
requiredSettings.value: Office 365 (or specifically SharePoint Online + Exchange Online)

requiredSettings.label: Session
requiredSettings.value: Use app enforced restrictions (gives browser-only, no download/print/sync on unmanaged devices)

requiredSettings.label: Companion setting
requiredSettings.value: SharePoint admin center → Policies → Access control → Unmanaged devices = 'Allow limited, web-only access'

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA12 - App-enforced restrictions - Unmanaged browser').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = Office 365.
Conditions → Client apps → Configure = Yes → tick 'Browser'; Done.
Access controls → Session → tick 'Use app enforced restrictions'; Select.
In SharePoint admin center set Unmanaged devices to 'Allow limited, web-only access' so SharePoint/OneDrive enforce the browser-only experience.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create; switch to On after validation.

requiredSettings.label: Conditions — Device platforms
requiredSettings.value: Configure = Yes; Include = Any device; Exclude = Windows, macOS, iOS, Android (the platforms you support)

steps: New policy → name it (e.g. 'CA13 - Block - Unsupported platforms').
Assignments → Users → Include = All users; Exclude = break-glass accounts.
Target resources → Include = All resources.
Conditions → Device platforms → Configure = Yes → Include = Any device → Exclude = Windows, macOS, iOS, Android (deselect any platform you don't support); Done.
Access controls → Grant → Block access; Select.
Set Enable policy = Report-only, Create. Pair with a compliance/app-protection policy (platform is user-agent based and can be spoofed), then switch to On.
